title: Computer Organisation: CPU, Memory, I/O, Buses
summary: Describes the main hardware components of a computing system and the von Neumann model.
content_points: Von Neumann: memorised program; instructions and data in same memory; PC tracks next instruction.
Main components: CPU (processes), memory (stores), I/O peripherals (communicate), buses (connect).
Three buses: data (transfers data/instructions), address (selects location), control (CPU signals).
CPU executes instructions indicated by PC; PC updates to next or branched address.
learning_objectives: List the main components of a von Neumann computing system
Describe the purpose of each system bus
